title: anthurium sursumtepalum
intro: Anthurium sursumtepalum, also known as Queen of the Night, is a rare and unique flowering plant species belonging to the Arum family, characterized by its vibrant white and green flowers that bloom at night and wilt before dawn. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, particularly in the Philippines, where it is believed to possess medicinal properties that can help in reducing inflammation and fever. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its ability to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Anthurium sursumtepalum can be used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from various health conditions.
